textByHashes.19ac229d950375a7cba4cde6518f88f634c76975: There be nothing 'so-called' about curses, laddie!
textByHashes.884481c902180e4f889c1a2f6d18a45607b026f3: There be nothing 'so-called' about curses, lassie!
textByHashes.10a56a68a53f26807b0154cbdfd3c6597dfbbd6a: There be nothing 'so-called' about curses, young'un!
textByHashes.1cfe3c51478509f8803da38ae5c70bc35094edec: Arrr, get away with ye, ye scurvy scallywag!
textByHashes.b5f526ad12dc7c2601a381152397e324ec557bb0: Arrr, ye may be wanting to go to Crandor Island now, but if ye gets there, ye'll be a-wantin' to get off it again quickly enough!
textByHashes.403574c04cb7c003a85062061cb78599e11febd8: There's only one sailor alive daft enough to go there. Old Ned, the ropemaker in Draynor Village. I can't think of any other fools just now. Except for old Jack Seagull there.
textByHashes.0c98427aac138ff25409351c3d508e53042d4c47: Nay, always ye say it wrong! Tis 'we are', not 'we be'.
textByHashes.939b0d957983d1abe084d4df4ec861cd44fb6eaf: Ye be a fool, and a disgrace to piracy.
textByHashes.983b755a0b2ca23828b83c172c21d777270fd948: Redbeard Frank ye say? He be outside. Says he likes the feel of the wind on his cheeks.
textByHashes.3af6c2ab952cc53b3474ccda18def1c8c757f3d6: I was to be marooned, ye see. A scurvy troublemaker had taken my ship, and he put me ashore on a little island.
textByHashes.6de5a5b57266238cb4fc422ad467fdbe3198decc: Arrr, ye see, he made one mistake! Before he sailed away, he gave me a bow and one arrow so that I wouldn't have to die slowly.
textByHashes.9187584f4a58c65c4cd4edec545796758b0258ee: So I shot him and took my ship back.
textByHashes.51a7c19580af6e3c010eff4d2d4de5a56aca12fe: Nay, but the barkeep hears most of the news around here. Or Redbeard Frank, he's often spoken of buried treasure. Perhaps ye should be asking them for quests.
textByHashes.90661ed24a54d601f9a51f1922346eb214d79439: Nay, but Redbeard Frank has often spoken of buried treasure. Perhaps ye should be asking him for a quest.
textByHashes.29df69b62aee2cfee4da35563b03c75bf4c1df90: Nay, but the barkeep hears most of the news around here. Perhaps ye should be asking him for a quest.
textByHashes.f74c2345671143bfa4b70c908f0b78a6a9f6ff46: Nay, I've nothing for ye to do. But I hear there's an old landlubber in Draynor Village who's always a-looking for a lively lad to do him a favour.
textByHashes.3663697f8790beb44d55a16876f23121694b9001: Nay, I've nothing for ye to do. But I hear there's an old landlubber in Draynor Village who's always a-looking for a lively lass to do him a favour.
